prompt: Create a vertical museum-gallery scene showing an original anime-style girl as an ultra-detailed three-dimensional paper craft artwork bursting out from a large white shadow-box frame mounted on a wall. The artwork is a single oversized framed portrait: 1 young girl figure, 1 deep rectangular white frame, 1 small museum visitor standing at the lower right for scale, and 1 plain square privacy-like block covering the center of the girl’s face. The girl has soft ash-brown long wavy hair made from layered cut paper strips, delicate anime proportions, a sleeveless cream dress with many folded paper ruffles, a pale blue ribbon tie, and a small bracelet. Surround her with intricate paper flowers, translucent petals, pale blue leaves, curling ribbon strips, and layered floral cutouts that extend beyond the frame, creating the feeling that the paper artwork is coming alive and spilling into the room. Use pale sky blue accents among mostly ivory, beige, cream, and light blush tones. The setting is a quiet contemporary art museum with a high wall, wooden floor, soft natural sunlight from an upper-right skylight, long shadows, and a lone visitor in a black coat looking up at the piece. Visual style: photorealistic museum installation mixed with refined anime illustration, extremely precise paper-cut texture, visible folded edges, layered cardstock depth, delicate shadows, translucent vellum-like pieces, elegant pastel color grading, cinematic lighting, high detail, whimsical yet realistic. Composition: centered giant paper portrait occupying most of the image, visitor small in the bottom-right corner, frame slightly protruding from the wall, flowers and ribbons extending outward in shallow relief. Constraints: no text, no watermark, no extra people, keep the square face block plain and centered, maintain a vertical 4:5 aspect ratio.
